data.title: bd-28dr.1: Merge engine collect step
data.description:
Implements the collect step (Step 1) of the N-way merge pipeline.

## What was implemented

- **src/merge/mod.rs**: Module definition and re-exports
- **src/merge/types.rs**: Core types — ChangeKind, FileChange, PatchSet
- **src/merge/collect.rs**: collect_snapshots() — collects changed-file snapshots from all source workspaces

## Key design decisions

- PatchSet::new() sorts changes by path on construction for determinism guarantee
- Empty workspaces produce empty PatchSet (not filtered out) — caller decides how to handle
- Deletion-only workspaces are fully supported (is_deletion_only() flag)
- collect_snapshots() calls backend.snapshot() + backend.status() per workspace, then reads file content from the workspace directory for add/modify changes
- Fail-fast: first workspace error returns Err immediately

## Tests

20 tests cover: error display, empty workspace, add/modify/delete changes, deletion-only workspaces, 3-workspace scenario with various patterns, order preservation, content correctness, nonexistent workspace error.

All tests pass.

data.reason: Security review of the collect step (src/merge/collect.rs, src/merge/mod.rs). NOTE: review anchor commit 6509e319 is absent from this git repo's object store (created under jj SCM, never imported), so seal diff/comment could not resolve — reviewed current source directly. Findings: no exploitable vulnerabilities. No injection surface (uses gix write_blob/read_blob_at_path, not shell, despite 'git hash-object' docstrings). Symlink handling is correct & defensive (reads link-target text via read_link, never follows links for content — prevents smuggling arbitrary target bytes into blobs). No auth/crypto/secrets in this path. One LOW defense-in-depth item only (not blocking): read_workspace_file_and_mode joins ws_path.join(rel_path) trusting the backend to return worktree-relative paths; git backend never emits absolute/'..' paths so not exploitable, but an explicit rel_path.is_relative()/no-ParentDir guard would harden the trust boundary. LGTM.
